channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Elon Musk’s xAI startup just bought X for $45 billion
item.description: Elon Musk said on Friday that his xAI has acquired X, the social media app formerly known as Twitter, in an all-stock transaction for $45 billion, including $12 billion in debt. “xAI and X’s futures are intertwined.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Ubuntu namespace vulnerability should be addressed quickly: Expert
item.description: Linux admins who have enabled the unprivileged user namespace restriction in their recent Ubuntu environments should take action to close three new vulnerabilities that allow a threat actor to bypass the supposed protection.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A Nvidia chip shortage is coming, warns major Chinese server maker
item.description: One of China’s largest server makers, H3C, has flagged potential shortages of Nvidia’s H20 chip, the most advanced AI processor legally available domestically under U.S. export controls, in a client notice seen by Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: ChatGPT's viral Studio Ghibli-style images highlight AI copyright concerns
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fans of Studio Ghibli, the famed Japanese animation studio behind “Spirited Away” and other beloved movies, were delighted this week when a new version of ChatGPT let them transform popular internet memes or personal photos into the distinct style of Ghibli founder Hayao Miyazaki.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk: Trump will 'go after' people 'pushing the lies' about Tesla
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk suggested Thursday that the Trump administration will crack down on vehicle vandalization, expressing his concerns about protesters targeting Tesla dealerships across the U.S. and abroad.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: La Cámara de Cuentas de Andalucía moderniza su sistema de gestión financiera
item.description: La Cámara de Cuentas de Andalucía da un paso adelante en la modernización de sus sistemas de gestión económica y financiera para optimizar la operativa contable y los trámites administrativos presupuestarios.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: La transformación digital, esencial para la supervivencia del sector asegurador
item.description: Los beneficios de la transformación digital ya son palpables para el sector asegurador. Así lo constata el informe Perspectivas de tecnología en seguros 2025: transformando la innovación en ventaja competitiva que firma Concentrix.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Samsung confirms Galaxy Watches aren't tracking sleep properly – here's the fix if you're affected
item.description: Samsung has confirmed that the Galaxy Watch has a sleep-tracking problem. Here's what you need to do.
item.dc:creator: stephen.warwick@futurenet.com (Stephen Warwick), TechRadar

item.title: Meet the DOGE team
item.description: Several members of Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) made their first public appearance as a team to make the case for their cost-cutting efforts amid growing pushback.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: What is 'classified' information? What are 'secure' communications? Here's a primer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's use of a popular messaging app to discuss sensitive military plans — with a journalist on the text chain — is raising questions about security and the importance of safeguarding the nation's secrets.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Adobe Introduces Experience Platform Agent Orchestrator for Complex Decision-Making
item.description: Adobe introduces the Experience Platform Agent Orchestrator, a reasoning engine that enables AI agents to perform complex decision-making and problem-solving tasks, accelerating customer experience orchestration.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: With the rise of genAI, it’s time to follow Apple’s Security Recommendations
item.description: Apple’s Safari browser has a really useful password management feature, which is now also available as a standalone app called Passwords.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Teams successful on 52.2% of ball/strike challenges during spring training robot umpire test
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Teams won 52.2% of their ball/strike challenges during the spring training test of robot umpires, and catchers had a markedly better success rate than pitchers.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump family crypto firm launches stablecoin
item.description: World Liberty Financial, a cryptocurrency venture created by President Trump and his sons, announced plans Tuesday to launch a stablecoin. The stablecoin, USD1, will be pegged to the U.S. dollar. Stablecoins are cryptocurrencies tied to assets, like fiat currency, to maintain a more stable price.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Red Hat’s AI portfolio evolves to address enterprise deployment challenges
item.description: Red Hat announced updates to Red Hat OpenShift AI and Red Hat Enterprise Linux AI (RHEL AI), with a goal of addressing the high costs and technical complexity of scaling AI beyond pilot projects into full deployment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: DOGE staffer allegedly ran company providing services to hacking group
item.description: US Department of Government Efficiency tech advisor Edward Coristine previously ran a small infrastructure provider that offered services to a cybercriminal group, it has been alleged.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Commercial innovations to drive FedRAMP’s new approach
item.description: Pete Waterman, the director of the cloud security program known as FedRAMP, said four new community working groups will help give industry a bigger voice. The post Commercial innovations to drive FedRAMP’s new approach first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Napster sold to tech commerce company for $207 million
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A brand that was notoriously connected to music piracy before reemerging as a subscription music service has been sold to Infinite Reality for $207 million.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Waymo plans to bring its driverless taxis to Washington in 2026
item.description: Waymo on Tuesday added Washington to its pioneering robotaxi service's steadily expanding list of U.S. markets, although passengers will have to wait until next year until they can take a driverless ride around the nation's capital city.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Feds Need Reliable Systems for Return-to-Office Mandate
item.description: Federal government agencies spend $2 billion annually to operate and maintain federal office buildings and $5 billion on office leases, whether they’re being used or not.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: 20 powerful women shaping the networking industry
item.description: Women are severely underrepresented in top leadership roles across the business world. Only 10.4% of the Fortune 500 companies have women CEOs. In an AP survey of S&P 500 companies, only 25 of 341 CEOs were women. That disparity extends into the technology sector.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A European startup scrubs its attempt to launch an orbital rocket on its first test flight
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — A private European aerospace company scrubbed its attempt on Monday to launch the first test flight of its orbital launch vehicle from Norway.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: World Backup Day is March 31
item.description: Are you ready? World Backup Day is March 31, a day that has been recognized every year since it was first set up in 2011.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: Coverage of Nvidia GTC 2025 With its legacy of innovation in GPU technology, Nvidia has become a dominant force in the AI market. Nvidia’s partners read like a technology who’s who list – e.g.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: DoorDash to offer eat first, pay later option to customers
item.description: DoorDash and Klarna are partnering to offer consumers a buy now, pay later option when ordering food delivery.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese robot's kung fu moves will make your jaw drop
item.description: A humanoid robot developed in China has transformed from a nimble dancer to performing kung fu moves with surprising precision and balance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Protect your files and memories with simple spring cleaning tips
item.description: To protect memories, documents and personal data, you should back up your information on personal drives, according to Kurt the CyberGuy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
